raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I made a series of payments totaling over {$1600.00} toward my American Express credit card. On XX/XX/XXXX, Amex applied XXXX {$200.00} payments, XXXX of which they now claim was a duplicate credit caused by a system error, even though both appeared on my statement at the time. 

I was never notified of any issue or over-crediting. I continued using my account and paying off my balance in full. My XXXX XX/XX/XXXX balance was {$0.00}, and I stopped using the card after that. Suddenly, in XX/XX/XXXX, Amex added a {$200.00} charge, claiming it was a reversal of that duplicated XXXX credit. 

I escalated the issue, explained that I relied on their reporting and acted in good faith, and provided my statement showing how the payments were reflected. Amex offered only a {$50.00} goodwill credit, leaving me responsible for the remaining {$150.00} due to a mistake that was entirely on their end. They ended up not giving me the {$50.00} statement either. 

This is not a charge I made or authorized, its a retroactive fix for something their system did months ago, which misled me into thinking I had paid more than I had.
